perturbation.original_text: 3. Freight and payment method: (a) Verification of freight: Party A pays freight based on carload rate (such freight includes pick-up charges, door-to-door delivery charges and tax fees). (b) Party A shall not pay any other charges other than the freight. (c) Clearance of freight: the clearance method is based on both Parties' fund clearance arrangement and the final clearance amount is subject to actual carriage amount and EX-warehouse ("EXW") weight determined by Party A. Party B shall attach Party A's parcel EXW originals or copies for Party A's verification for clearance of freight.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Freight and payment method: (a) Verification of freight: Party A pays freight based on carload rate (such freight includes pick-up charges, door-to-door delivery charges and tax fees). (b) Party A may be responsible for additional charges including, but not limited to, fuel surcharges and handling fees, as determined by Party B. (c) Clearance of freight: the clearance method is based on both Parties' fund clearance arrangement and the final clearance amount is subject to actual carriage amount and EX-warehouse ("EXW") weight determined by Party A. Party B shall attach Party A's parcel EXW originals or copies for Party A's verification for clearance of freight.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause (b) explicitly states that Party A shall not pay any other charges other than the freight. The modified clause (b) introduces the possibility of additional charges (fuel surcharges and handling fees) determined by Party B, directly contradicting the previous statement that there are no other charges creating uncertainty on what needs to be paid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Freight and payment method: (a) Verification of freight: Party A pays freight based on carload rate (such freight includes pick-up charges, door-to-door delivery charges and tax fees). (b) Party A may be responsible for additional charges including, but not limited to, fuel surcharges and handling fees, as determined by Party B. (c) Clearance of freight: the clearance method is based on both Parties' fund clearance arrangement and the final clearance amount is subject to actual carriage amount and EX-warehouse ("EXW") weight determined by Party A. Party B shall attach Party A's parcel EXW originals or copies for Party A's verification for clearance of freight.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated Party A 'shall not pay any other charges other than the freight.' The changed text indicates Party A 'may be responsible for additional charges including, but not limited to, fuel surcharges and handling fees, as determined by Party B.' This creates a direct contradiction as the original text prohibits additional charges while the modified text allows them, thus causing in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 15. Any different interpretation of this Agreement by both Parties is subject to final interpretation by Party A.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. Any different interpretation of this Agreement by both Parties shall be resolved through binding arbitration. The arbitration decision will be final and binding on both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text grants Party A the sole authority to interpret the agreement, implying they have the final say in any disputes. The modified text mandates binding arbitration to resolve disputes, meaning a neutral third party will make the final decision. This directly contradicts Party A's unilateral interpretive power and it will cause uncertainty in conflict resolution.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any different interpretation of this Agreement by both Parties shall be resolved through binding arbitration. The arbitration decision will be final and binding on both parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text gives Party A the final say in interpreting the agreement, whereas the changed text mandates binding arbitration for resolving disputes, making the arbitration decision final. The Contradicted Text and Changed Text are the same, but the Changed Text contradicts the Original Text, which leads to a contradiction. This creates a direct contradiction regarding dispute resolution, because it is unclear which party's decision should be considered final, hence, causing a strong contradiction.
